task.title: Improve MCP/Pi prompt so LLMs naturally use priority and blocked_by
task.body:
LLMs using the MCP or Pi extension tend to skip priority and blocked_by unless explicitly told to set them. Rewriting prompts so setting these fields feels obviously correct.

AUDIT RESULT:
- internal/cli/cli.go has segmentsShortcutInstructions (SessionStart inject) and mcpServerInstructions (MCP initialize) and mcpToolDefs() descriptions
- internal/cli/segments.ts has tool descriptions + before_agent_start hook
- CRITICAL BUG FOUND: segments.ts uses 0-3 low-high scheme ("0=none, 1=low, 2=medium, 3=high") while MCP/CLAUDE.md uses 1=urgent/2=normal/3=backlog. This is inverted and must be unified.

BEADS RESEARCH (from steveyegge/beads AGENTS.md + beads-mcp server.py):
- Concrete parenthetical scenarios per priority tier beat abstract labels ("security, data loss" vs "high")
- Inline -p 1 / --deps examples in every command block
- "Ready queue" idiom for unblocked tasks as a decision primitive
- Named dep types ("discovered-from") for follow-ups found mid-task
- Imperative CAPS + negative instructions ("Do NOT create markdown TODO lists")

CHANGES:
1. segments.ts: unify priority to 1/2/3 urgent/normal/backlog, rewrite blocker text with correctness framing, pull ready-queue into before_agent_start output
2. cli.go segmentsShortcutInstructions: add scenario-based priority cues, frame blocked_by as correctness not hint, add "discovered-from" pattern, add ready queue footer
3. cli.go mcpServerInstructions: same tightening
4. cli.go mcpToolDefs: priority param description gets scenario cues, blocked_by gets "REQUIRED when A cannot start until B lands" language
5. cli.go runContext: split todos into Ready vs Blocked sections per project
6. Tests: cover ready-queue split (pure function) and verify priority string contains 1/2/3 urgent/normal/backlog cues

STATUS: implementation in progress.

task.title: Graph view: make 'ready queue' filter show tree + blockers, not just ready tasks
task.body:
Problem: When the 'Ready queue' filter is active and the user switches to graph view, the graph is nearly empty / uninformative. Reason: `filteredTasks()` applies `Util.ready()` globally, and a ready task by definition has no unresolved blockers (see web/index.html line 2400 + Util.ready at line 2069). So the graph receives only unblocked todos, with no edges -> no tree to show.

User intent: the list view interpretation of 'ready' (only actionable items) is correct for a to-do list, but in the graph view 'ready' should mean 'show me the in-flight project tree leading up to what's ready -- include blocked/blocker/in_progress tasks, hide the completed noise'. That way the graph visualizes WHY things are/aren't ready.

Proposed behavior:
- Graph view, when Store.filter === 'ready': include tasks with status in {todo, in_progress, blocker}, plus any task that is a transitive blocker of those (climb blocked_by chains). Exclude done + closed.
- List view: unchanged.
- Visually mark which nodes in that set are themselves 'ready' (reuse the existing ready ring class, which already exists -- see line 2685 `<rect class="ring" ...`).

Where:
- web/index.html
  - `filteredTasks()` line 2398: don't apply ready filter when the current view is graph -- let each graph fn decide. Or add a `viewFilteredTasks(view)` helper.
  - `Util.ready()` line 2069 (keep as-is for list).
  - obsGraphView() line 2655, conGraphView() line 3027, dosGraphView() line 3397: when Store.filter === 'ready', compute the tree-aware subset described above and render that; still highlight the 'ready' nodes within it.
  - graph header text (e.g. line 2700 'N ready / M total') should reflect the graph's subset, not the whole project.

Done when: with ready filter on, graph view shows the in-flight dependency tree (not a blank canvas), with ready nodes visually marked; done/closed tasks stay hidden; toggling ready off still shows the full graph; `make build` succeeds.

Related (not blocking): focus-mode task d46b4daf (they compose -- focus mode narrows to one branch within this filtered view).

task.title: Build Graph view (dependency DAG, theme-aware)
task.body:
Add Graph view showing the dependency DAG.

Reference: AGraphView in direction-a-app.jsx (lines ~405-503). SVG-based. Rank = longest path. Nodes colored by status. Edges = blocked_by. "Ready" nodes (todo with all blockers done) get an accent glow ring.

Work:
- Shared layout math (rank + position).
- Each theme renders nodes/edges with its own palette.

Acceptance: graph renders the current tasks' DAG; clicking a node opens detail panel; ready tasks are visually distinct.

task.title: Verify fix in a fresh claude-code session
task.body: Verified: fresh Claude Code session shows mcp__segments__* tools in the deferred tools list and the server instructions ("Segments tracks multi-session work as a dependency graph...") are being injected. MCP handshake works end-to-end.

task.title: Graph view: filter panel (priority and blocker filters)
task.body:
Add a filter panel to the graph view.

Filters to support:
- Priority: show/hide nodes by priority level (multi-select: 1, 2, 3, unset)
- Blocker relationship: toggle to show only tasks that have blockers or are blocked by something (i.e. nodes with at least one edge)

When a filter hides a node that is part of a visible edge, either hide the edge too or grey it out -- be consistent. Filtered-out nodes should not just vanish and leave dangling edges.

Filter state persisted to localStorage.

File: web/index.html (single-file UI, all JS inline). Graph is rendered client-side (existing canvas/SVG logic).

task.title: Full keyboard shortcuts (g+l/k/g, 1-4 priority, t cycle status)
task.body:
Flesh out keyboard handling beyond the basic j/k/esc/slash that Obsidian ships with.

Reference: keyboard useEffect in SegmentsA (lines ~774-832 of direction-a-app.jsx).

Shortcuts to add:
- g then l/k/g -- switch to list/kanban/graph view (two-key chord; 800ms window).
- 1/2/3/4 with a task selected -- set priority to P0/P1/P2/P3 (not P1..P4 -- design says 1 key => p0).
- t with a task selected -- cycle status todo -> in_progress -> blocker -> done -> closed -> todo.
- e -- open edit/detail panel for selected.
- c -- open inline composer (already shipped).
- / -- focus search (already shipped).

Ignore key events while inputs are focused (except esc).

Acceptance: all documented shortcuts work; no conflicts with inputs.

task.title: Web UI hangs under live WebSocket update load
task.body:
Web UI at http://127.0.0.1:8765 hangs (can't click, can't swap views) under even 1 event/sec from `bin/stress.exe`. Root cause: every WebSocket message triggers a full `app.innerHTML = …` rebuild.

## Hot spots in `web/index.html` (3889 lines)

1. **WS handler ~lines 2149-2217** calls `sortAndRender()` / `render()` synchronously per `task:created|task:updated|task:deleted`. No batching.
2. **`render()` ~line 3612** does `app.innerHTML = html` — full DOM wipe + rebuild. ~1500 nodes for a 30-task project.
3. **`Util.ready()` / `Util.counts()` called 2-6x per render** at lines 2494, 2633, 2839, 3005, 3217, 3375. Each rebuilds `byId` from scratch and scans all tasks.
4. **`obsSidebar()` / `conSidebar()`** (2547, 2913) filter `Store.tasks` per project inside a map — O(projects × tasks).
5. **`Graph.layout()`** (~2401) does recursive rank traversal every graph render.
6. **`obsDetail()`** (2745) finds dependents by filtering all tasks per render.

## Priority fix (unblocks everything else)

Add rAF coalescing to the WS handler only:

```js
let _pending = false;
function scheduleRender() {
  if (_pending) return;
  _pending = true;
  requestAnimationFrame(() => { _pending = false; render(); });
}
```

Swap every `render()` / `sortAndRender()` inside lines 2149-2217 (the WS handler) for `scheduleRender()`. Keep synchronous `render()` for user-driven events (click, keypress) so UI stays snappy to input.

That alone turns a 100-msg burst into 1-2 rebuilds.

## Follow-up optimizations (order by payoff)

- Compute `byId`, `ready`, `counts` once at render start, pass to subrenderers.
- Pre-bucket tasks by project_id in the store; update incrementally on task:created/updated/deleted.
- Memoize `Graph.layout()` on a cheap tasks-hash.
- Reverse index `dependentsOf[taskId]` in the store.

## Repro

- `./segments start` (or `segments.exe start`)
- Open http://127.0.0.1:8765
- `./bin/stress.exe` (defaults: 1 agent, 8s rate, 20 max)
- Try to click a task or change views — UI freezes.

Found while building `cmd/stress/main.go` (task f02cfbab).

task.title: List view: basic filtering (status, priority, text search)
task.body:
Add a filter bar to the task list view.

Filters to support:
- Status: multi-select checkboxes or toggle pills (todo, in_progress, done, closed, blocker)
- Priority: multi-select (1, 2, 3, unset)
- Text search: freeform input, matches against title and body

Filters compose (AND logic). Active filters should be visually indicated. A "clear all" affordance to reset.

Filter state persisted to localStorage (ties in with UI state persistence task 548d2f4f).

File: web/index.html (single-file UI, all JS inline). Filtering is purely client-side.

task.title: Implement Console theme (Direction B) in web/index.html
task.body:
Add Console theme to the Themes registry scaffolded by 339c8731-768d-4f6f-9c06-e8d6385fae93.

Source: C:\Users\Adam\dev\segments\.design-spec\project\direction-b-app.jsx + Segments - B Console.html.

Characteristics:
- Warm black bg #0a0b08, amber text #d7c89a, phosphor-green highlights.
- JetBrains Mono + IBM Plex Mono.
- Row vocabulary: ASCII brackets `[~]` for status, `!!!` ramps for priority, inline ASCII for blockers.
- seg-blink, seg-pulse-b, seg-enter-b animations.
- Agent tail log below list (terminal feel).

Work:
- Add `[data-theme="console"]` token set to CSS block in web/index.html.
- Add `console` entry to Themes registry with its render function + glyph map.
- Verify theme picker switches cleanly between obsidian and console.
- Respect same component contract (topbar/sidebar/list/detail/composer).

Acceptance:
- Switching theme picker to "console" re-skins the whole app without page reload.
- All existing CRUD still works.
- No console errors.

task.title: Reinforce blocked_by guidance in SessionStart hook for greenfield scaffolds
task.body: internal/cli/cli.go segmentsShortcutInstructions Blockers section. Observed failure: when scaffolding a greenfield project, the LLM did not mark the bootstrap task (e.g. 'Initialize SvelteKit project') as a blocker for all the downstream pages that literally cannot render without it. The existing wording mentions the pattern but the LLM still missed it. Strengthen: explicit rule - 'In a greenfield scaffold, the initial bootstrap/install task is almost always blocked_by for every downstream task; use #0 in segments_create_tasks to point to it.' Keep the section short.

task.title: Dossier sidebar: drop Views section, keep only stamp stickers
task.body: In dosSidebar() in web/index.html, remove the '— Views —' side-label and the Ready queue text-button (.side-item with data-act='toggle-ready'). The only way to toggle the ready filter stays the big red Ready-queue stamp (.ready-box) which is already wired to data-act='toggle-ready'. Adjust spacing so the Projects section is the first thing in the sidebar. Rebuild + install.

task.title: Implement new segments web UI: Obsidian theme + theme picker infrastructure
task.body:
Rebuild web/index.html as a multi-theme task tracker UI. Start with ONE theme fully implemented (Obsidian / Direction A), plus theme-picker infrastructure that makes it trivial to add Console (B) and Dossier (C) later.

Design source files (read these):
- C:\Users\Adam\dev\segments\.design-spec\project\Segments - A Obsidian.html
- C:\Users\Adam\dev\segments\.design-spec\project\direction-a-app.jsx (877 lines, React)
- C:\Users\Adam\dev\segments\.design-spec\project\shared\direction-a-components.jsx (208 lines)
- C:\Users\Adam\dev\segments\.design-spec\project\shared\seed.js
- C:\Users\Adam\dev\segments\.design-spec\chats\chat1.md (design brief)

Rules:
- Vanilla JS only. No CDN at runtime. Single self-contained web/index.html.
- Backend is unchanged: REST endpoints in internal/server/server.go, WS hub in ws.go, data model in internal/models/models.go. Task.BlockedBy is a single string (not array) in our backend; design shows array -- use single.
- //go:embed picks up web/index.html; build with `make build`.
- No emojis in code. No AI boilerplate comments. Terse.

Architecture:
- `<body data-theme="obsidian|console|dossier">` switches theme; theme tokens scoped under `[data-theme="X"]`.
- Shared JS: Store (state), API (fetch), WS, Util (relTime, firstLine, counts, ready), Render dispatcher.
- `Themes` registry: { obsidian: { render, glyphs, fonts }, ... } -- each theme owns its render function.
- Theme picker persists choice in localStorage `segments.theme`.
- Preload Google Fonts for all 3 themes (Geist, Geist Mono, JetBrains Mono, IBM Plex Mono, Instrument Serif, Space Grotesk).

Obsidian (A) feature checklist for THIS task:
- Topbar: logo + version, view tabs (List/Kanban/Graph -- only List works now), search input, palette-button (stub), WS indicator, theme picker, light/dark toggle.
- Sidebar: All tasks + Ready queue + Projects list with per-project status sparklines (AProjectBadge in components file).
- List view: grouped by status, with priority-bar glyph, status pill (colored glyph + label), ID in mono, title, body preview, blocker chip, relative time.
- Detail panel: right slide-in, shows metadata grid + markdown-ish body + Blocked-by / Blocking lists.
- Composer: inline create (press `c` or click +) -- title + priority + optional blocker.
- Create/update/delete/create-project all work via existing API.
- Basic keyboard: j/k navigate, enter open, esc close/deselect, / focus search.
- Light mode token set present (Obsidian has both dark and light per design).

Explicitly deferred (separate tasks):
- Console theme (B)
- Dossier theme (C)
- Kanban view
- Graph view
- Command palette
- Full keyboard (g+l/k/g, 1-4 priority, t cycle status)

Expected outcome:
- web/index.html is fully self-contained Obsidian-styled UI with theme picker UI visible in topbar.
- `make build` succeeds.
- Existing CLI writes (sg add / done / update) broadcast via WS and show up live.
- Existing create/edit/delete flows from prior UI still work (feature parity).

task.title: Dossier: add green All-tasks stamp under Ready queue stamp
task.body: When the multi-project All-tasks aggregated view lands (task 4642b413), add a second big stamp sticker in the Dossier sidebar beneath the red Ready-queue stamp, styled in stamp-green (--seg-done) with a slight rotation offset so they feel like two stacked stickers. Should toggle/switch into the all-tasks view. Parity with how the red Ready stamp works today.

task.title: Graph view: render task priority visually on nodes
task.body:
Problem: Graph nodes currently show status (color bar) and title but no priority indicator. User wants to see priority at a glance in the graph across all 3 themes.

Goal: Encode priority (1=urgent, 2=normal, 3=backlog, 0=unset) visually on each g-node. Proposals (pick one that fits each theme's language):
- Small priority chip in a corner of the node (`P1` / `P2` / `P3`, hidden or muted for 0).
- Left border thickness or accent color keyed by priority.
- Outline glow for P1.

Theme-specific suggestions:
- obsidian: subtle accent-colored dot or chip top-right; P1 gets a stronger ring.
- console: `[P1]` / `[P2]` / `[P3]` text tag in monospace near the title; P1 in amber/red.
- dossier: typographic label (e.g. roman numeral or `PRI-1`) in the meta line.

Where:
- web/index.html
  - obsGraphView() line 2655, CSS block 468+
  - conGraphView() line 3027, CSS block 1088+
  - dosGraphView() line 3397, CSS block 1856+
- Task.Priority is already on the task object (models.go: `Priority int`).

Done when: every node in every theme conveys priority without needing to click; P0 is visually neutral (don't shout about unset); `make build` succeeds.

task.title: Multi-project All-tasks aggregated view
task.body:
Add a real 'All tasks' mode that aggregates tasks across every project.

Server work:
- New endpoint: GET /api/tasks?all=1 (or equivalent) that iterates all project LMDB envs and returns a flat list. Include project_id on each task.
- store.ListAllTasks() helper that fans out across projects.

Client work:
- When 'All tasks' sidebar link is clicked, set Store.activeProjectId=null and a flag like Store.scope='all'.
- loadTasks() branch: if scope='all', call the new endpoint; otherwise per-project.
- List row gets a small project-name chip on the right (already see project color in sidebar badges; reuse that token).
- Detail panel shows 'Project: <name>' row in metadata grid.

Acceptance: toggling 'All tasks' shows every task across every project, annotated with origin. Creating from this view still needs a target project selector in the composer.

Related: item 2 in the 'Obsidian polish' task -- this is the concrete follow-up if we choose path (a).

task.title: Refresh README for current state (post-kanban + favicon)
task.body:
Bring README.md up to date after recent work. Include:
- All three themes (including the newly built Kanban view + favicon)
- Accurate install / run / build sections
- Current CLI + MCP surface
- Cross-platform support (Windows)
- Any notable recent additions since last README pass

Style: keep it clean and readable. Insert the new favicon SVG as a pseudo-logo at the top if it fits nicely. Do not add AI-attribution footers.

task.title: Persist UI state across browser refresh
task.body:
Persist key web UI state to localStorage so it survives a page refresh. State to persist:
- Selected project ID
- Active view (list vs graph)
- Whether "ready queue" filter is active
- Any other view-level toggles added in the future

On load, restore from localStorage before first render so there is no flash of default state. On each state change, write to localStorage. Key prefix: `segments.ui.*` to avoid collisions.

File: web/index.html (single-file UI, all JS is inline)

task.title: Git-workflow JSONL export (Beads-parity snapshotting)
task.body:
Add optional JSONL export of task state to a configurable path on disk so the git tree can capture task snapshots alongside code changes, matching Beads' git-workflow compatibility without relying on an LLM to manually export.

Motivation:
- Beads' core workflow leans on exporting tasks to JSONL so commits carry the task state. Segments should support the same pattern so users who want task history tracked in-repo can get it for free.
- Today, to get that you'd have to script it externally. Make it first-class.

Design sketch:
- New config keys in `~/.segments/config.yaml` (and per-project override via `sg init` / project meta.json):
  - `jsonl_export.enabled: bool`
  - `jsonl_export.path: string` (e.g. `.segments/tasks.jsonl` relative to project root, or absolute)
  - `jsonl_export.on_events: [created, updated, done, closed, deleted]` (subset; default = all write events)
  - `jsonl_export.scope: project|all` (single project vs. all projects in one file)
- Writer lives in `internal/store` or a new `internal/export` package. Called from the same write paths that already fire `notifyServerEvent` in `internal/cli/cli.go` and the HTTP handlers in `internal/server/server.go`, so CLI / MCP / web writes all snapshot consistently.
- Format: one JSON object per line, full Task struct + a small envelope `{"event": "task:updated", "ts": "...", "task": {...}}`. Matches the WSMessage shape already in use for easy reuse.
- On every configured event, append to the file. Provide `sg export jsonl [--path ...] [--project ...]` for a full-snapshot dump on demand (useful for first-time adoption / rebuilds).
- Consider a `sg import jsonl <path>` counterpart for round-trip (replay a JSONL into the store). Nice-to-have, not required for v1.

Constraints:
- Must not block write path: export is best-effort, errors logged but not fatal.
- File writes need to be safe under concurrent CLI + server writes (append + fsync, or queue through a single goroutine).
- Honor the same project_id resolution rules used elsewhere (CWD basename / single-project / env).
- Windows-safe paths (use filepath, not path).

Expected outcome:
- User enables `jsonl_export` in config, points it at `.segments/tasks.jsonl` inside their repo, commits normally. Every `sg done`, `sg add`, MCP update, and web edit appends a line. Git diffs show task state changes alongside code changes. No LLM scripting required.

Relevant files:
- internal/cli/cli.go (write paths: add/done/close/update)
- internal/server/server.go (HTTP write handlers + /internal/sync)
- internal/store/store.go (central write point -- likely the best hook site)
- internal/models/models.go (Task/envelope types)
- web config surface unchanged for v1 (YAML only).

task.title: MCP server: stop responding to JSON-RPC notifications
task.body: internal/cli/cli.go mcpServer loop Encoded a response for every decoded message, including JSON-RPC notifications (no id). Per MCP/JSON-RPC 2.0 spec, servers MUST NOT send any response to a notification. notifications/initialized from claude-code triggered an error reply {code -32601}, likely the root cause of claude-code not registering segments tools. Fix: if req has no id field, skip enc.Encode.

task.title: Invert and emphasize priority scale (1=urgent, 2=session work, 3=backlog)
task.body: internal/cli/cli.go segmentsShortcutInstructions Priority section AND mcpToolDefs() priority field descriptions AND mcpServerInstructions. Current scale is inverted from what user wants: current says 3=high/urgent, but user wants 1=urgent/NOW, 2=regular session work, 3=backlog/future. 0 stays as "unset/legacy". Also emphasize: when CREATING tasks during planning, LLM MUST choose 1/2/3 based on intent, do not default everything to 0. Update all three locations (SessionStart text, tool schema descriptions, server-side instructions field) consistently.

task.title: SessionStart hook: clarify tool naming, drop Pi line, fix CLI fallback snippet
task.body: internal/cli/cli.go segmentsShortcutInstructions. Now warns: client may expose tools with or without mcp__segments__ prefix; trust the client tool list; do not invent names. CLI fallback snippet no longer requires -p (since CLI now auto-resolves). Pi line removed from generic SessionStart context.

task.title: Graph view: strengthen visual distinction for blockers
task.body:
Problem: Tasks with status=blocker, and edges from a blocker, aren't visually loud enough in the graph. User wants blocker state to read clearly at a glance across all 3 themes.

Current state:
- console + dossier already differentiate `.edge.open` vs `.edge.blocked` (blocked edges use accent/red). obsidian uses a single edge style.
- Nodes with status=blocker use `--seg-blocker` on the status bar but aren't otherwise distinguished.

Goal:
1. Make blocker-status nodes stand out more (e.g. stronger outline, fill tint, or subtle pulse/glow). Not just a color bar.
2. Ensure edges originating from (or pointing at) blocker tasks use the `.blocked` edge style in all 3 themes -- obsidian currently doesn't split open/blocked edges; add that.
3. Arrowheads should match their edge style (already true in console/dossier; add to obsidian).

Where:
- web/index.html
  - obsidian graph CSS lines 468-520, render fn obsGraphView() line 2655
  - console graph CSS lines 1088-1145, render fn conGraphView() line 3027
  - dossier graph CSS lines 1856+, render fn dosGraphView() line 3397

Done when: scanning the graph, blocker nodes and blocked edges are unmistakable in all 3 themes without hovering/clicking; `make build` succeeds.

Coordinate with: focus-mode task (out-of-branch dimming must not hide the blocker emphasis for in-focus nodes).

task.title: List view: sortable columns (priority, UUID, last modified)
task.body:
Add sort controls to the task list view, works across all themes.

Sort options:
- Priority (ascending/descending)
- UUID (ascending/descending -- useful for stable ordering)
- Last modified date (ascending/descending)

UI: clickable column headers or a sort dropdown -- whichever fits the existing list layout without a redesign. Active sort column should have a visible indicator (arrow or similar). Default sort should remain whatever it currently is (sort_order / created_at).

Sort state should be persisted to localStorage (ties in with the UI state persistence task 548d2f4f).

File: web/index.html (single-file UI, all JS inline). Sorting is client-side; data is already in memory.

task.title: Blocked-by field: fuzzy searchable combobox with active-task filter
task.body:
Replace the plain text input for blocked_by in the web UI task editor with a searchable combobox.

Behavior:
- Clicking the field opens a dropdown immediately showing all candidate tasks
- Typing filters by fuzzy match against task title and UUID prefix simultaneously
- Matches ranked: title prefix > title substring > fuzzy > UUID prefix
- Completed tasks (status: done, closed) are hidden by default; a toggle (e.g. "show finished") reveals them
- Selecting an entry fills the field with the task UUID (display the title as the label)
- Escape or click-outside closes the dropdown without committing
- Keyboard navigable (arrow keys + Enter)

File: web/index.html (single-file UI, all JS inline). The task list is already loaded in memory from the API; no new network calls needed for filtering.

Cleanup to fold in (currently a known issue):
- Long blocker option labels in the existing native `<select>` get clipped without ellipsis inside the detail pane (e.g. "339c8731 — Implement new segments web UI:" cuts off mid-word). Browsers don't apply text-overflow to `<select>` children. The combobox replacement should render its selected value as a plain `<div>` (or button) so we can use `text-overflow: ellipsis` AND/OR a tooltip (`title=`) showing the full label. Same applies to the composer's blocker-select. The interim CSS workaround (overflow-wrap/word-break) doesn't help `<select>` — only the redesign does.

task.title: Bug: MCP segments_create_task + segments_update_task drop priority field
task.body:
Observed 2026-04-19: calling `segments_create_task(title, body, priority=2)` returned the task with `priority:0`. A follow-up `segments_update_task(task_id, priority=2)` also returned `priority:0` (and a subsequent get confirmed it stuck at 0). The batch endpoint `segments_create_tasks([{..., priority: 2}])` worked correctly on the same session, same project, same body.

Repro:
1. Call `segments_create_task` with a non-zero integer priority -> response shows priority:0.
2. Call `segments_update_task` on that id with priority=2 -> response still priority:0, `updated_at` advances (so the write path ran), but the field didn't change.
3. Same payload via `segments_create_tasks` batch -> priority persists correctly.

Likely location: internal/cli/mcp handler for the single-task create/update. Probably a JSON tag mismatch or a zero-value omit issue (e.g. `omitempty` on a field that should accept 0 explicitly, OR the handler reads priority from the wrong key, OR there's a default-zero overwrite before persisting).

Compare:
- Single-task create handler
- Single-task update handler
- Batch create handler (which works) -- diff the priority-handling lines

Done when: `segments_create_task(title="x", priority=2)` returns priority:2 and persists; `segments_update_task(id, priority=3)` returns priority:3 and persists; add a unit test in internal/cli if there's a test harness for MCP handlers, or extend an existing one. `CGO_ENABLED=1 go test ./...` passes.

task.title: Pick design language and produce mockups before coding UI
task.body: Before touching web/index.html, produce 2-3 mockups using Anthropic's frontend design tool (Claude Artifacts / Claude for Design). Evaluate on: density, clarity of status/priority/dependencies, kanban-readiness, dark theme feel. Pick one, iterate, THEN implement. Save mockups in design/ directory at repo root (create it).

task.title: Build Kanban view (theme-aware)
task.body:
Add Kanban view to all three themes (blocked on 339c8731 landing first).

Reference: AKanbanView in direction-a-app.jsx (lines ~308-403). Columns: todo / in_progress / blocker / done. HTML5 drag-drop between columns triggers PUT /api/tasks/{id} with new status. Optimistic update + WS reconciliation.

Work:
- Expose `view=kanban` in the view tab row.
- Each theme provides its own Kanban render (column header, card style, placeholder).
- Drag-drop handlers in shared JS (theme-agnostic).

Acceptance: drag a card between columns; status updates persist; WS broadcasts to other tabs.

task.title: "Mark all as seen" button to clear unseen-flash state
task.body:
Add a button (e.g. in the project header or filter bar) that marks every currently-flashing task as seen in one click, for users who do not want to hover over each task individually.

Behavior:
- Clicking it writes seen-timestamps for all tasks (or at least all non-in_progress tasks) to localStorage, stopping all active pulse animations immediately
- Button should only be visible / enabled when there is at least one unseen task; hide or grey it out otherwise
- Label suggestion: "Mark all seen" -- keep it short

Depends on the unseen-flash persistence task being implemented first.

File: web/index.html (single-file UI, all JS + CSS inline).

task.title: Implement Dossier theme (Direction C) in web/index.html
task.body:
Add Dossier theme to the Themes registry scaffolded by 339c8731-768d-4f6f-9c06-e8d6385fae93.

Source: C:\Users\Adam\dev\segments\.design-spec\project\direction-c-app.jsx + Segments - C Dossier.html.

Characteristics:
- Paper-cream #efece4 bg, ink-black text #161312, rubber-stamp red #a8291f for blockers.
- Instrument Serif (display) + Space Grotesk (body).
- Oversized numerals for priority, typographic ornaments for status.
- Asymmetric editorial grid.
- seg-blink-c, seg-pulse-c, seg-enter-c animations.

Work: same as Console task -- add CSS + Themes registry entry + render fn.

Acceptance: same as Console -- theme swap works, CRUD works, no errors.

task.title: MCP initialize: declare tools capability, echo client protocolVersion, add instructions
task.body: internal/cli/cli.go handleMCP case initialize. Was: capabilities:{} and hardcoded protocolVersion 2024-11-05. Now: declares capabilities.tools.listChanged=false, echoes client protocolVersion if supported (2025-06-18, 2025-03-26, 2024-11-05), adds instructions field with short Segments usage pointer. Also handles prompts/list and resources/list with empty arrays for clients that probe them. Error code changed from string -32601 to integer -32601 per JSON-RPC 2.0.

task.title: Web UI render perf follow-ups (post-rAF-coalescing)
task.body:
Follow-up optimizations for `web/index.html` after the rAF-coalescing fix in 108b7a30 landed. The coalescing collapses bursts into ~1 render per frame; these reduce the cost of each render. Priority 3 because the browser no longer hangs; only pursue if render profiling shows it's needed on larger projects.

Order by payoff:

1. **Pass `byId`, `ready`, `counts` through render context.** Currently each theme (`renderObsidian` ~line 2493, `renderConsole` ~2838, `renderDossier` ~3216) calls `Util.counts(Store.tasks)` + `Util.ready(Store.tasks)` at the top, and subrenderers re-call `Util.ready(...)` (lines 2633, 3005, 3375) and `Util.counts(ptasks)` inside `obsSidebar`/`conSidebar` (2550, 2915, 3291). Each `Util.ready` rebuilds `byId` from scratch (Util at ~line 2069). Compute once per render, thread through as an arg.

2. **Pre-bucket `Store.tasksByProject` in the store.** `obsSidebar`/`conSidebar`/`dosSidebar` (~2547, 2913, ~3270) filter `Store.tasks` per project inside a `.map(projects, ...)` = O(projects * tasks). Maintain an index in `upsertTaskLocal` / `task:deleted` handlers and in `loadTasks()`.

3. **Memoize `Graph.layout()` (~line 2401).** Invalidate on a cheap hash of `[id, blocked_by]` pairs so graph view doesn't re-rank on every unrelated re-render.

4. **Reverse index `Store.dependentsOf[taskId]`.** `obsDetail` (2745) / `conDetail` / `dosDetail` find dependents via `Store.tasks.filter(t => t.blocked_by === task.id)` every render. Maintain incrementally alongside tasks.

No user hang reported after (1)-(4) would be needed; drop this task if it never surfaces as a real bottleneck.

task.title: Add issue templates (bug + feature request) for Gitea and GitHub
task.body:
Segments now carries a WIP disclaimer pointing users at the issue tracker, so we need proper issue templates to funnel useful reports. The repo lives on Gitea but is mirrored to GitHub, so both need templates.

Scope:
- Create `.gitea/issue_template/` with YAML-form templates (Gitea supports the same schema as GitHub issue forms).
- Create `.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/` with the same templates for GitHub.
- Two templates at minimum:
  1. `bug_report.yaml` -- fields: OS + arch, install method (script / source / release binary), `sg version` output, affected command (CLI / web UI / MCP / Pi), steps to reproduce, expected vs. actual, relevant logs (`~/.segments/server.log` if applicable), screenshot optional. Labels: `bug`.
  2. `feature_request.yaml` -- fields: problem context, proposed behaviour, alternatives considered, whether the user is willing to contribute a PR. Labels: `enhancement`.
- Optional third: `question.yaml` pointing discussion-style asks to wherever we want them (tracker is fine for now).
- Add a config file (`config.yaml` / `config.yml`) in each location with `blank_issues_enabled: false` and a `contact_links:` pointer to the README's Status section, so the template path is the only friction-free entry.

Notes:
- Gitea looks in `.gitea/issue_template/` OR `.gitea/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/`. Pick lowercase for consistency with the existing `.gitea/workflows/`.
- GitHub looks in `.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/`. Use uppercase to match GitHub convention.
- Keep the forms short. We want more reports, not fewer. Do not require fields that are easy to skip.
- `sg version` should be a direct copy-paste field rather than asking for commit SHA, since releases inject version via ldflags.
- Cross-reference the templates from the README Status section ("open an issue" link could instead go to `/issues/new/choose`) once they land.

Files:
- `.gitea/issue_template/bug_report.yaml` (new)
- `.gitea/issue_template/feature_request.yaml` (new)
- `.gitea/issue_template/config.yaml` (new)
- `.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/bug_report.yaml` (new, same content)
- `.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/feature_request.yaml` (new, same content)
- `.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/config.yml` (new)
- `README.md` -- update the "open an issue" link target in the Status section to `/issues/new/choose` after templates are live.

Expected outcome:
- `New Issue` on both Gitea and GitHub shows a picker with Bug / Feature options, each with a structured form. Blank issues are disabled. Most incoming bugs arrive with enough context to triage without a back-and-forth.

task.title: Add `sg rm <task_prefix>` CLI command for parity with segments_delete_task
task.body: internal/cli/cli.go. Currently there is no CLI command to delete a task; the MCP has segments_delete_task but the CLI does not have an equivalent. During the priority/blocker fix session I had to bounce back to MCP with full UUIDs to clean up test tasks because sg has no delete-task command. Add `sg rm <task-id-prefix>` that uses findTaskByPrefix for resolution, calls store.DeleteTask, and notifies via notifyServerEvent("task:deleted", ...). Consider `sg rm --project <name>` as an alternate spelling for the existing remove-project command, or give them separate names (`sg rmproj` vs `sg rm`). Also add `delete` as an alias.

task.title: Graph view: focus/isolate selected task's tree when clicked
task.body:
Problem: When a task is clicked in the graph view, all other unrelated nodes/edges stay at full prominence, so a selected task that has blockers/dependents feels cluttered -- the user can't easily see the branch that matters. Reported across all 3 themes.

Goal: On click (selection), dim or fade nodes/edges that are not in the selected task's connected component (upstream blockers + downstream dependents, transitively). The selected branch should pop; everything else should recede (e.g. opacity ~0.18-0.25, or grayscale). Clicking empty space / deselecting returns to full view.

Where:
- web/index.html, three render fns:
  - obsGraphView() around line 2655
  - conGraphView() around line 3027
  - dosGraphView() around line 3397
- Each already applies a `.sel` class to the selected g-node. Extend with an `.off` (or similar) class for out-of-branch nodes/edges, styled per-theme in the CSS blocks near lines 468+, 1088+, 1856+.

Implementation sketch:
1. Build adjacency (both directions) once per render from the tasks' blocked_by.
2. When selection changes, BFS from the selected id in both directions -> set of in-focus ids.
3. Toggle class on SVG nodes and edges based on membership. No re-layout needed.
4. Preserve existing selection behavior (detail pane, keyboard nav).

Done when: clicking a task with blockers/dependents visibly isolates that subtree in all 3 themes; clicking empty area restores the full graph; no console errors; `make build` succeeds.

Related follow-ups (separate tasks): priority rendering, blocker emphasis.

task.title: Dossier: soften dark-mode rules, restyle agent + mode switcher
task.body:
User feedback after initial Dossier implementation:
1. Dark mode has harsh white lines/borders separating elements. Root cause: --seg-rule = #eae2d0 in dark mode (matching light-mode ink-on-paper contrast). Fix by softening dark --seg-rule to muted parchment (#6f675a-ish) so 2px dividers feel like aged ink, not white lines.
2. "Agent Live" blinking red looks out of place for olde-dossier aesthetic. Recolor to ochre (--seg-inprog), slow the animation to a gentler opacity fade (segInkFadeC), switch label to italic serif.
3. Dark/Light mode switcher (boxy uppercase "LIGHT"/"DARK" button) looks modern-HUD. Replace with italic serif lowercase "day"/"night" with dotted underline, no border box.

Files: web/index.html (also copy to internal/server/index.html), rebuild + install.
Acceptance: dark-mode Dossier looks ink-on-aged-paper (no bright white 2px frames), agent indicator fades softly in ochre, mode switcher reads as a typographic annotation.

task.title: CLI: auto-resolve project_id in sg add / done / close via resolveProjectIDForMCP
task.body: internal/cli/cli.go runAdd now calls resolveProjectIDForMCP(s, hint) when -p omitted. runDone/runClose factored into resolveProjectAndTaskArgs which supports 1-arg form (sg done <task_prefix>) finding the task globally via findTaskByPrefix, and 2-arg legacy form (sg done <project_hint> <task_prefix>). Task ID prefix support added to 2-arg path too.

task.title: Strengthen create_tasks schema description to prevent stringified-array bug
task.body: internal/cli/cli.go mcpToolDefs() segments_create_tasks entry. Some LLMs stringify the tasks array instead of passing a real JSON array (observed: one session sent "tasks": "[\n {\n ..." instead of "tasks": [...]). Add to the description: 'tasks MUST be a JSON array of objects, NOT a JSON-encoded string.' Same hint on the outer description. Also consider adding an example in the description or tightening the schema with minItems: 1.

task.title: Obsidian polish + small UX gaps
task.body: Consolidated backlog of small gaps left after the Obsidian MVP landed. All 8 items resolved. Disposition: 1. Composer blocker field -> DONE. Added blocker

task.title: sg export: default to current project only, add --all for cross-project dump
task.body:
Rework `sg export` defaults in `internal/cli/cli.go` (`runExport`, ~line 1212) so the bare command produces a git-friendly per-project index, and a cross-project dump is an explicit opt-in.

## Desired behavior

1. `sg export` (no flags): export ONLY the auto-resolved current project (same resolution order used elsewhere: explicit `--project`/`-p` hint -> `$SEGMENTS_PROJECT_ID` -> CWD basename match -> single-project fallback). Default path `.segments/tasks.jsonl` (relative, lands in the working tree for git tracking). Error out if no project can be resolved rather than silently dumping everything.
2. `sg export --all`: export every project. Default path should live under the global data dir, e.g. `~/.segments/tasks.jsonl` (use `expandPath(dataDir)` / `filepath.Join(expandPath(dataDir), "tasks.jsonl")`, NOT a relative path that would pollute CWD).
3. `--path <file>` / `-o <file>`: always wins, for both single-project and `--all` modes. `export.ResolvePath` already handles `~` and relative paths.
4. `--project <id|name>` / `-p`: still works, restricts to one project (overrides the CWD auto-resolve). Mutually exclusive with `--all` -- error if both are passed.

## Config interaction

`jsonl_export.path` from `~/.segments/config.yaml` currently pre-empts the built-in default (see `runExport` lines 1238-1246). Keep honoring it as an override, but only when `--path` is not given and the user has not passed `--all` (since `--all` has its own home-dir default). Document this in `--help`.

## Help text

Rewrite the `-h/--help` block (lines 1226-1230) to match:

```
usage: sg export [--path <file>] [--project <id|name>] [--all]
  (default)    export the current project to .segments/tasks.jsonl (git-friendly)
  --all        export all projects to ~/.segments/tasks.jsonl
  --path, -o   override output path (works with default and --all)
  --project    restrict to a specific project by UUID prefix or name
```

## Tests

Add coverage in `internal/cli/cli_test.go` (or new file) for:
- bare `sg export` in a dir whose basename matches a project exports only that project
- bare `sg export` with ambiguous resolution returns the same "cannot auto-resolve" style error used by MCP
- `sg export --all` writes to the home-dir default when no `--path` is given
- `--path` override beats both defaults
- `--all` + `--project` is rejected

## Out of scope

Do not change the streaming `jsonl_export` hook config semantics (`internal/export/export.go` Writer/Config). This task is only about the on-demand `sg export` CLI command defaults.

task.title: Add favicon using Obsidian theme logo
task.body:
Add a favicon to the web UI. Reuse the logo/mark shown in the Obsidian theme's view. Wire it up in web/index.html via a <link rel="icon"> tag. Prefer an SVG data URI or inline SVG to keep the single-file-HTML constraint intact; fall back to a small embedded PNG/ICO only if SVG does not render as a favicon.

Acceptance: favicon appears on the tab for both dev server and production builds. No extra file fetches if we can avoid them.

task.title: Graph view: scroll selected node into view (avoid overlay panel hiding it)
task.body:
Now that the detail panel is `position: absolute` and overlays the right ~380-440px of `.content`, clicking a g-node that lives in that strip can hide it under the panel. Mouse selection currently doesn't scroll the graph; only `Actions.navigate()` (j/k) passes `scrollTo: '.row.selected, .g-node.sel'` to render().

Fix: in `Actions.selectTask(id)` (web/index.html ~line 2300), when `Store.view === 'graph'`, call render with `scrollTo: '.g-node.sel'` so the selected node is scrolled into view. Account for the overlay panel width: ideally the selected node lands LEFT of the panel, not under it. Could either (a) just call scrollIntoView with `inline: 'center'` and trust horizontal scroll, or (b) compute the panel width and use `scrollIntoView` then nudge the .graph scroll left by panel width.

Done when: clicking a node on the right edge of the graph leaves the node fully visible, not under the detail panel. List + dossier + console themes.

task.title: Build cmd/stress web UI stress demo
task.body:
Standalone stdlib-only HTTP client that simulates a swarm of agents building DAGs of `[Stress]`-prefixed tasks against the live Segments server so we can visually stress-test list/kanban/graph views.

Files:
- `cmd/stress/main.go` (new)
- `Makefile` (add `stress` target)

Flags: `-project` (default UUID 67011e8b-10f5-4ea7-94da-8840e3e0b5d9), `-agents` (6), `-host` (127.0.0.1:8765), `-rate` (2s), `-max` (80), `-cleanup-only`, `-keep`.

DAG shapes: chain(4), fan-out(4), diamond(4), tree(7). Walk nodes in topo order, todo -> in_progress -> done with 300-1500ms jitter; 10% chance of an in_progress -> blocker -> in_progress side-trip (800-2500ms). Priority weighted 10% P1 / 60% P2 / 30% P3. Titles sampled from a pool.

API: POST /api/projects/{id}/tasks to create, PUT /api/tasks/{id} to set blocked_by + status (send priority: -1 to preserve). BlockedBy is a single string in the API; diamond walker waits on all deps internally even though only one edge is persisted.

Cleanup on SIGINT/SIGTERM (and on panic via defer): list all, filter `[Stress]` prefix + body contains `stress-run-id: <runID>` (unless -cleanup-only, which nukes all [Stress]* across runs), compute blocked_by-chain depth, DELETE in descending depth so leaves go first.

Console: per-transition line `HH:MM:SS  agent=N  task=abcd...  shape  Title  from->to`. Summary every 10s: live / created / completed / cleanup-pending.

task.title: Task flash persists until hovered (unseen-changes indicator)
task.body:
Currently tasks flash briefly on update/completion. Change this so the flash state is "sticky" -- it keeps pulsing/highlighting until the user hovers over that task, at which point it is marked as seen and the animation stops.

Scope:
- Applies to all statuses EXCEPT in_progress (in-progress is already visually active; no point flagging it)
- Primary use case: user comes back after a session, sees at a glance what changed (done, todo modifications) without relying on dates

Implementation notes:
- Track seen state in localStorage keyed by task UUID + updated_at timestamp (or a hash thereof). If updated_at advances past the stored value, the task is "unseen" again.
- On hover (mouseenter), mark the task as seen and stop the animation
- The flash animation should be subtle but persistent -- a slow pulse or a left-border accent, not a jarring rapid blink
- On page load, any task whose updated_at is newer than its stored seen-timestamp gets the unseen style immediately (no flash delay)

File: web/index.html (single-file UI, all JS + CSS inline).

task.title: Build Command palette (cmd/ctrl+k)
task.body:
Add cmd/ctrl+k command palette.

Reference: APalette in direction-a-app.jsx (lines ~607-681). Shows actions (switch view, toggle theme, mark selected done/in_progress) + task jump list. Fuzzy-filter over label + sub.

Work:
- Modal overlay; esc/click-outside closes.
- Input focused on open; arrow keys navigate; enter runs selected.
- Theme-agnostic shell; each theme styles it via CSS vars.

Acceptance: cmd+k opens palette; typing filters; enter runs action or jumps to task.
